items.title: What is wild game processing?

expanded_element.title: Wild Game Processing | Minnesota Department of Agriculture
expanded_element.description: Wild game processing is any additional handling or preparing of field-dressed wild game, from cutting and skinning a carcass, to curing, cooking, or smoking. Wild game processors conduct these activities as a commercial business.

items.title: What is the average cost to get a deer processed?
Who are the big 4 beef processors?

expanded_element.title: 'Big Four' meatpackers under fire as beef prices soar - The Guardian
expanded_element.description: Tyson is one of four beef producers – along with JBS, Cargill and National Beef, known collectively as “the Big Four” – that control 85% of the industry, and their profit margins are at their highest levels in years as consumer prices soar.

items.title: Do deer processors make good money?

expanded_element.title: Deer Processing Salary: Hourly Rate April 2026 USA - ZipRecruiter
expanded_element.description: While ZipRecruiter is seeing annual salaries as high as $73,500 and as low as $28,000, the majority of Deer Processing salaries currently range between $41,000 (25th percentile) to $58,500 (75th percentile) with top earners (90th percentile) making $67,000 annually across the United States.
